pages_summary.description: Anchor bolt details and shimming specifications
pages_summary.extracted_text: BASE UNIT BASE PLATE, 20.00, 1.50, 1.50, WASHER, 5.50, .90, NUT, 1/2" PLATE THICKNESS, 8, 9.25, 3/8" REF, TOP OF FLOOR ELEVATION, ANCHOR DETAILS & SHIMMING, 16.00 TYP, 11.50 30.00, 3" MINIMUM EMBEDMENT (GREATER EMBEDMENT PROVIDES ADDITIONAL ANCHORAGE STRENGTH), 8, 7.25, LEVELING SHIM, ANCHOR BOLT LOCATION DIMENSIONS AND DETAILS, .90, CONCRETE THICKNESS SEE GENERAL NOTES, ANCHOR BOLT, ANCHOR BOLT DETAIL, 1" MAXIMUM LEVELING SHIM THICKNESS SEE NOTE 10, 1" MAXIMUM LEVELING SHIM THICKNESS SEE NOTE 10, INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS, C OF BASE, 1-14 R, DRILL THE HOLE PERPENDICULAR TO THE WORK SURFACE, ASSURE FULL HOLDING POWER. DO NOT REAM THE HOLE OR ALLOW THE DRILL TO WOBBLE, 8", 8", DRILL THE HOLE DEEPER THAN THE INTENDED EMBEDMENT OF THE ANCHOR, BUT NO DEEPER THAN 1/2 THE OVERALL DIAMETER OF THE ANCHOR MEASURED TO THE BOTTOM (OPPOSITE) SURFACE OF THE CONCRETE, .31 R TYP, PLACE LEVELING SHIMS IN A STRAIGHT AND ORDERLY FASHION AT EACH ANCHOR BOLT, USE THIN SHIMS TO FULLY FILL OUT EACH LOCATION, CLEAN THE HOLE USING COMPRESSED AIR AND WIRE BRUSH. A CLEAN HOLE IS NECESSARY FOR PROPER PERFORMANCE, TURN THE NUT ONTO THE ANCHOR UNTIL CONTACT IS MADE WITH THE TOP OF THE SPREADER AND THE BOTTOM OF THE WASHER. INSERT ANCHOR INTO HOLE, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 1, 6, REPRESENTATIVE TIGHTENING SEQUENCE FOR ANCHOR BOLTS TIGHTENING FROM CENTER OF BASE OUTWARD, TAP ANCHOR INTO HOLE WITH A 2 1/2 LBS HAMMER UNTIL WASHER SETS FLUSH WITH NUT TIGHTENING, 2.94, TIGHTEN THE NUT TO 95 FT-LBS MAXIMUM TORQUE AND NOT LESS THAN 3 FULL REVOLUTIONS. NEVER TURN FASTER THAN ONE TURN EVERY FIVE SECONDS, 1.43, APPROVED ANCHOR BOLTS PROVIDED BY MOHAWK LIFT, ANCHOR BOLTS ARE MANUFACTURED BY WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS 2415 EAST 13TH PLACE TULSA, OKLAHOMA 74104, TYPICAL ANCHOR BOLT, 1-14, PHONE 918-747-7444 OR 800-343-1264, THE MAXIMUM THICKNESS OF ANY STACK OF SHIMS IS 1" SEE NOTES 9 & 10, 2.26, WEB SITE WWW.WEJ.COM, PLACEMENT OF LEVELING SHIM DETAIL, LEVELING SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES FROM 1/16", 1/8", & 1/4, ANCHORS SPECIFIED ARE: "THE ORIGINAL WEJ-IT" EXPANSION ANCHORS, 3/4 DIA, LEVELING SHIM DETAIL, CATALOG NUMBER LENGTH, 3450 6", 3462 8 1/2", 3410 10", ALWAYS WEAR SAFETY GLASSES, FOLLOW THE DRILL MANUFACTURER'S SAFETY RECOMMENDATIONS FOR CARBIDE-TIPPED DRILL BITS MEETING ANSI B212.15 DIAMETER STANDARDS

pages_summary.description: Lift data table and required materials list
pages_summary.extracted_text: LIFT DATA TABLE, MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD, PARALLELOGRAM LIFT MODEL 75-48-SURFACE, LIFT UNIT DATA, MAXIMUM LOAD CAPACITY (LBS) 75,000, ANCHORAGE, ANCHOR BOLT DIAMETER (IN.) 3/4", TOTAL NUMBER OF ANCHOR BOLTS 84, BOLT PATTERN SEE ANCHOR DETAILS, ANCHOR BOLT SETTING TORQUE N/A- SEE ANCHOR DETAILS, MINIMUM EMBEDMENT LENGTH (IN.) 3.00, MINIMUM CONCRETE THICKNESS (IN.) 6" ON GRADE (SEE NOTE 23), HYDRAULICS, RESERVOIR CAPACITY (GAL) 30 TOTAL, OIL TYPE DEXRON III (ATF), ELECTRICAL, MOTOR HORSEPOWER 20, 208/230, 3 PH 60 AMPERE, 460 V, 3 PH 30 AMPERE, CONTROL CIRCUIT TRANSFORMER 1000 VA 7.6" AMP, 24 VDC POWER SUPPLY 4.5 AMP, LIGHT FIXTURES (OPTIONAL LIGHTING KIT) QTY 8, SHOP AIR, AIR PRESSURE (PSI) 85 to 100, AIR VOLUME- LIFT (CFM)(LOCKS) -, AIR VOLUME- OPTIONAL ROLLING JACK (CFM) 25 EACH, AIR VOLUME- OPTIONAL TURNTABLE (CFM) -, AIR VOLUME- TOTAL REQ'D CAPACITY (CFM) 30 MINIMUM, AIR VOLUME- TOTAL REQ'D CAPACITY (CFM) 50 SUGGESTED, REQUIRED MATERIAL LIST, MATERIALS SHOWN ON THIS LIST SHALL BE USED WITHOUT SUBSTITUTION UNLESS SPECIFICALLY APPROVED IN WRITING BY MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD., 12 LR LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX PER LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODES, 14 LR LEVELING SHIMS WEJ-IT 1/8" + WEDGE ANCHORS, 9 4 1" LEVELING SHIM WEJ-IT METRIC, 8 4 1" SEAL BARRIER CROUSE - HINDS EYS3, 7 4 1-3/4" REDUCER BUSHING CROUSE - HINDS REX2, 6 1 SCH 40-90 DEG ELBOW CROUSE - HINDS EL3, 5 AR JUNCTION BOX (4" SQ) STEEL, 4 AR SEALTITE FLEXIBLE CONDUIT METAL CORE, 3 AR 1" RIGID CONDUIT STEEL, 2 AR 4" SCH 40 UNION/REGULATOR, DRYER SHUTOFF, 2 AR 4" SCH 40 STREET ELBOW STEEL or PVC, 1 AR 4" SCH 40 PIPE STEEL or PVC, ITEM QTY DESCRIPTION MATERIAL, ITEMS SUPPLIED BY MOHAWK WITH THE LIFT UNIT

GENERAL NOTES, NOTE 1, IF THE EXISTING CONCRETE FLOOR HAS A DOCUMENTED MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c= 4,000 psi AND IS AT LEAST 6 INCHES THICK, THEN THE FLOOR SYSTEM MAY BE USED WITHOUT ALTERATION. GENERALLY ANY FLOOR AREA WHICH IS DESIGNED FOR VEHICLES EQUAL TO OR GREATER THAN THE LIFT UNIT MAXIMUM CAPACITY WILL BE ADEQUATE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, LIFT UNIT CONTACT PRESSURES ON THE FLOOR ARE LESS THAN THE WHEEL CONTACT PRESSURES, NOTE 2, IF THE CONCRETE FLOOR SYSTEM DOES NOT MEET MINIMUM SPECIFICATIONS OF NOTE 1 ABOVE, THEN A NEW CONCRETE FLOOR SHALL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE LIFT, NOTE 3, IF THE STRENGTH OF AN EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM IS UNKNOWN OR NOT DOCUMENTED, ITS STRENGTH SHOULD BE DETERMINED. CORE SAMPLES SHOULD BE TAKEN TO DETERMINE THE STRENGTH OF THE FLOOR, NOTE 4, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM SHALL BE AT A MINIMUM F'c=4,000 psi, WITH HEAVY AGGREGATE, FOR ANY NEW CONCRETE IT SHALL REACH ITS FULL 28 DAY COMPRESSIVE STRENGTH BEFORE THE LIFT AND THE ANCHOR BOLTS ARE INSTALLED, NOTE 5, ANY NEW CONCRETE USED FOR FLOOR REPAIRS OR ALTERATIONS SHALL HAVE REINFORCING AS REQUIRED FOR THE SOIL CONDITIONS AND VEHICLE LOAD LEVEL. THE REINFORCING SHALL BE DETERMINED BY OTHERS. AT A MINIMUM #4 BARS AT 12" O.C. EACH WAY, BUT 6 10/10 WELDED WIRE FABRIC SHALL BE USED FOR ANY FLOOR REPAIRS. ALSO, FLOOR REPAIRS SHALL BE DOWELED INTO THE EXISTING FLOOR SYSTEM TO PREVENT DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT, NOTE 6, FOR INSTALLATION IN EXISTING STRUCTURES, AREAS OF THE FLOOR WHICH ARE CUT AND REMOVED FOR SERVICE LEG INSTALLATIONS SHALL BE REPAIRED WITH CONCRETE HAVING MINIMUM STRENGTH OF NOT LESS THAN F'c=4,000 psi AND AT LEAST 6" THICK IN THE AREAS SURROUNDING AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, NOTE 7, TO ALLOW FOR CONSTRUCTION, THE AREAS OF EACH FLOOR ALONG THE LIFT RUNWAYS SHOULD BE DEEPENED TO 9 INCHES FOR EASE OF ANCHOR INSTALLATION. ALSO, THE AREA OF THE SERVICE LEGS SHOULD PROVIDE A MINIMUM 9 INCH THICKNESS AROUND AND TO THE REAR OF THE SERVICE LEGS, FOR NEW CONSTRUCTION WHERE IN FLOOR RADIANT HEATING TUBES ARE USED, THESE TUBES MAY BE PLACED UNDER THE LIFT AREA PROVIDED THE THE SLAB IS CAST SUFFICIENTLY THICK. A MINIMUM OF 6 INCHES CLEARANCE SHOULD BE PROVIDED FOR ANCHOR BOLTS AND DRILLING ALLOTMENT. THE INSTALLER MUST BE NOTIFIED THAT RADIANT TUBES ARE TO BE USED AND MUST TAKE CARE IS TAKEN NOT TO OVER DRILL AND COMPROMISE THE DEPTH OF THE ANCHORS, NOTE 8, THE SUPPORT PLATES OF THE CONTINUOUS BASE SHALL NOT BE INSTALLED OVER A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OF THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE SUPPORT PLATES SHALL NOT BE PLACED NEARER THAN 10 INCHES FROM A CONSTRUCTION JOINT OR FREE EDGE OF THE FLOOR SLAB, NOTE 9, A MAXIMUM OF ONE INCH ANCHOR BOLT SHIM THICKNESS IS PERMITTED. INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE IN A RANGE OF THICKNESSES, NOTE 10, WHEN MORE THAN ONE INCH OF SHIM LEVELING IS REQUIRED FULL SUPPORT PLATE CONTACT SHIMS ARE AVAILABLE AT ADDITIONAL COST. THE FULL CONTACT SHIM PLATES SHALL THEN BE ACCURATELY LEVELED USING INDIVIDUAL ANCHOR BOLT SHIMS, NOTE 11, WEJ-IT FASTENING SYSTEMS, AT WEDGE ANCHORS ARE PROVIDED WITH THE LIFT FOR ANCHORING THE LIFT UNIT TO THE FLOOR SYSTEM. THE NUMBER AND THE SIZE OF ANCHOR BOLTS SPECIFIED IN THE DRAWING MUST BE USED TO ATTACH THE LIFT UNIT. ANCHOR BOLTS OF FULL LENGTH MUST BE USED AT ALL LOCATIONS PROVIDED ON THE BASE OF THE LIFT UNIT, NOTE 12, IN CERTAIN CASES THE FLOOR SLAB MAY HAVE ADEQUATE STRENGTH TO SUPPORT THE LIFT UNIT BUT MAY NOT BE THICK ENOUGH TO PROVIDE THE MINIMUM EMBEDMENT DEPTH FOR THE AT WEJ-IT ANCHOR BOLTS. IN THIS SITUATION EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS MAY BE USED, CONTACT MOHAWK RESOURCES, LTD. FOR WRITTEN APPROVAL OF EPOXY GROUTED ANCHORS AND PROCEDURES AND APPROVED METHODS FOR INSTALLATION OF THE LIFT UNIT, NOTE 13, EXCEPT AS DESCRIBED IN NOTE 2, NO EMBEDDED PLUMBING, TUBES, CONDUITS OR OTHER ITEMS, SHALL BE PERMITTED IN THE FLOOR UNDER THE LIFT UNIT INSTALLATION WHICH COULD BE DAMAGED BY ANCHOR BOLT HOLE DRILLING. THE SERVICE LEG CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED ACCURATELY IN THE LOCATIONS SHOWN IN SECTION PLAN AND DETAIL VIEWS TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT ON THE ANCHORAGE, NOTE 14, NO ANCHOR BOLT SHALL BE INSTALLED CLOSER THAN 10 INCHES FROM ANY FREE EDGE OR FLOOR JOINT, NOTE 15, PROVIDE TWO, 4 INCH SCH 40 PVC PIPE AS A HYDRAULIC-PNEUMATIC SERVICE SUPPLY CONDUIT RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO EACH SERVICE LEG, NOTE 16, PROVIDE 4, 1 INCH SCH 40 STEEL CONDUITS AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE POWER UNIT TO THE SERVICE LEGS. THESE CONDUITS SHALL BE INSTALLED AS SHOWN ON THE SECTION VIEWS AND MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES, NOTE 17, PROVIDE TEMPORARY CAPS FOR ALL CONDUITS AND EMBEDDED PIPES. IT IS RECOMMENDED TO LEAVE PULL ROPES IN CONDUITS FOR EASE OF LIFT INSTALLATION, NOTE 18, THE CONTROL CONSOLE MUST BE LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE LIFT IT SHOULD BE PLACED FAR ENOUGH AWAY FROM THE LIFT TO ALLOW FOR ACTIVITIES AROUND THE LIFT. THE CONSOLE DRAWINGS SHOW THE CONSOLE TO BE LOCATED ON EITHER SIDE AND ANYWHERE ALONG THE LENGTH OF THE LIFT, BUT ANY DEVIATIONS FROM THE ENCLOSED DRAWINGS MAY REQUIRE LONGER CABLES, HOSES, CONDUITS, OR ADDITIONAL EXPENSES TO THE PURCHASER, NOTE 19, THE LIFT REQUIRES 460 VOLT, 3 PHASE POWER SOURCE FOR LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE PROVIDED FOR THE POWER SOURCE. THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES. THIS ELECTRICAL DISCONNECTION IS TO BE PROVIDED BY OTHERS, NOTE 20, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS ELECTRICAL SERVICE SUPPLY RUNNING FROM THE BUILDING POWER SOURCE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND, ALTERNATIVELY IT MAY BE BROUGHT TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. PROVIDE A LOCKOUT/TAGOUT ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT BOX WITHIN 100 FEET AND AS CLOSE TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE AS IS PRACTICAL. THIS ELECTRICAL SUPPLY CONDUIT AND DISCONNECT BOX MUST BE INSTALLED ACCORDING TO LOCAL ELECTRICAL CODE REQUIREMENTS, NOTE 21, PROVIDE ONE, 1 INCH SCH 40 RIGID STEEL CONDUIT AS A COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY. THIS CONDUIT IS SHOWN UNDERGROUND RUNNING FROM AN AIR SOURCE TO THE CONTROL PANEL OVERHEAD, DEPENDING ON CUSTOMER PREFERENCE. IF BROUGHT OVERHEAD, PROVIDE FLEX CONDUIT CONNECTING AIR TERMINAL TO THE CONTROL CONSOLE, NOTE 22, THE LIFT UNIT REQUIRES CLEAN DRY COMPRESSED AIR AT THE PRESSURE AND VOLUME SHOWN ON THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR IS SUPPLIED WITH THE UNIT FOR THE LOCKING SYSTEM ONLY. A FILTER/LUBRICATOR/REGULATOR, AIR DRYER AND SHUTOFF VALVE MUST BE PROVIDED FOR THE LIFT UNIT TO OPERATE THE OPTIONAL ACCESSORIES. THE REQUIRED VOLUME OF AIR SHOWN IN THE LIFT UNIT DATA TABLE RECOGNIZES THAT NOT MORE THAN ONE AUXILIARY AIR CONSUMING DEVICE WILL BE USED SIMULTANEOUSLY, NOTE 23, ALL FLOOR REQUIREMENTS ARE BASED ON A CONCRETE SLAB THAT IS ON GRADE (SUPPORTED BY SOIL). ANY OTHER TYPE OF INSTALLATION INVOLVING A SLAB NOT ON GRADE (I.E.-SLAB SUPPORTED BY PYLONS, SECOND STORY SLAB, ETC.) MUST BE REVIEWED & ANALYZED FOR SUITABILITY BY THE BUILDING ARCHITECT, AT THE EXPENSE OF OTHERS, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, MOHAWK RESOURCES LTD., 75-48-SURFACE INSTALLATION REQUIREMENTS GENERAL NOTES
